rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ELIASON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFEDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Deft Moore Securities Company To Produce Its Custodian Of Records And Person Most Knowledgeable For Deposition, And To Produce Documents At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, January 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. PLAINTIFFS FONA ELIASON, ROBERT ELIASON, KARLENE ZUNIO, MARK ELIASSON'S MOTION To Compel Defendant Moore Securities Company To Produce Its Custodian Of Records And Person Most Knowledgeable For Deposition, And To Produce Documents At Deposition. Defendant¿s request for sanctions is denied. Defendant is required to produce a witness for deposition. If James Moore is unable to testify, Moore Securities may produce another, non-percipient witness who is qualified to testify. It is insufficient that James Moore testified in, and produced documents in other cases in which the Brayton firm represented the plaintiff, as the issues in the earlier cases and the present case may be different. Recognizing the difficulty that Moore Securities may have in producing documents from a company that has not been in operation since 1961, the Court orders the parties to meet and confer to determine what subjects relevant to this case were not adequately addressed in James Moore¿s earlier testimony and document production. Plaintiffs may then tailor their deposition and document requests for this specific case. The Court further suggests that the parties consider conducting an ¿in re complex litigation deposition¿ to prevent Moore Securities from having its witnesses repeatedly deposed on the same subjects. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re: Peition Of Margaret Patrick To Perpetuate Testimony Of Roger Patrick By Videotaped Deposition
